score_trend: 3.75 -> 3.5 -> 3.4 -> 3.1 -> 2.6. Fifth consecutive drop. Root cause: zero fixes shipped between reviews. Every re-opened item is accruing interest.
visually_confirmed_still_broken: Whiteboard 'FL OW' text wrap -- visible in mobile screenshot
Large dead space below office floor -- visible in mobile screenshot
ShortcutToast overlapping footer on mobile -- visible in mobile screenshot
Version v1.0.0-chaos in source, v1.0.5-chaos live -- both screenshots

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- Version drift: source v1.0.0-chaos, live v1.0.5-chaos
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #8/#9/#10. Live footer confirmed 'v1.0.5-chaos' in both screenshots; App.jsx footer hardcodes 'v1.0.0-chaos'. Five-patch gap. Either /workspace/src is perpetually stale (QA has been auditing dead code for months) or live patches are hand-applied without committing (catastrophic). Fix: (1) Sync /workspace/src to HEAD of deployed branch. (2) In vite.config.js: import pkg from './package.json' and add define: { '__APP_VERSION__': JSON.stringify(pkg.version) }. (3) Replace the hardcoded footer string with {__APP_VERSION__}.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- updated_at family #1: WorkerCard duration badge and StatsBar stuck detection never trigger
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #7/#8/#9/#10. Both features measure time since last API poll (updated_at resets every 30s) instead of task start. (1) WorkerCard lines 303-306: durationMs uses worker.updated_at, always <=30s, tier always 'normal', stuck badge never shows. Fix: replace worker.updated_at with worker.startedAt on lines 303, 304, and 306. (2) StatsBar useMemo ~line 386: filters stuck workers using w.updated_at > 7_200_000 -- genuinely stuck workers never surface. Fix: change w.updated_at to w.startedAt. Clean up stuckWorkers[0].updated_at fallback at ~line 457 by flipping the order once line 386 is fixed.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- updated_at family #2: char--deep-work aura and ghost desk visual never fire
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #10. (1) Office.jsx ~line 400: char--deep-work class (yellow glow + headphones badge) checks worker.updated_at > 45 min -- since updated_at resets on every poll this threshold never fires, zero deep-work auras ever shown. Fix: replace worker.updated_at with worker.startedAt. (2) Office.jsx lines 812-813: isGhostDesk checks occ.worker.updated_at > 900000 (15 min) -- same failure mode, translucent ghost desk never triggers. Fix: replace occ.worker.updated_at with occ.worker.startedAt. Total updated_at/startedAt confusion: 4 distinct broken features across two files.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- Office.css media query ordering confirmed: 768px block overrides 600px at 375px viewport
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #8/#9/#10. VISUALLY CONFIRMED in mobile screenshot: large blank area below the office floor. Source order lines 794, 831, 853 is: max-width:600px, then max-width:768px, then max-width:480px. At 375px ALL THREE match; later-in-source wins. The 768px block applies min-height:400px and aspect-ratio:auto to .office-floor AFTER the 600px block's 320px. The 480px block overrides min-height to 280px but never overrides aspect-ratio:auto -- creating the dead space. Fix: reorder to largest-first: 768px -> 600px -> 480px. Add aspect-ratio:unset inside the 480px block as a guard.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- Whiteboard renders 'FL OW' on mobile, confirmed in screenshot
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #7/#8/#9/#10. VISUALLY CONFIRMED in mobile screenshot: whiteboard shows 'FL' on one line and 'OW' on the next -- 'FLOW' broken mid-word by word-break:break-word at a ~17.7px container width. Fix (two-part): (1) Reorder media queries first (separate item above). (2) Add white-space:nowrap; overflow:hidden to .wb-vibe-text in Office.css so the text never wraps regardless of container width. Optionally shorten VIBE_WHITEBOARD['in-flow'] from 'FLOW' to 'GO' for extra safety margin.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- React.memo on WorkerCard defeated by stableActivityLog + side effect inside useMemo
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #7/#8/#9/#10. App.jsx lines 763-767: stableActivityLog = useMemo(() => { activityLogLenRef.current = activityLog.length; return activityLog }, [activityLog.length]). Two violations: (1) Any activity arrival invalidates stableActivityLog returning a new array reference -- ALL WorkerCards re-render on every new event, defeating React.memo entirely. Fix: const workerActivitySlices = useMemo(() => Object.fromEntries(activeWorkers.map(w => [w.id, activityLog.filter(e => e.worker === w.name).slice(-3)])), [activityLog, activeWorkers]) then pass activityEntries={workerActivitySlices[w.id] ?? []} per card. (2) Assigning activityLogLenRef.current inside useMemo is a React violation. The ref is never read in render. Delete that line.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- ShortcutToast untappable on mobile and clips beyond viewport, confirmed in screenshot
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #8/#9/#10. VISUALLY CONFIRMED in mobile screenshot: toast text clips at right edge and overlaps the footer. App.css still has pointer-events:none -- users cannot tap to dismiss for 7 full seconds on touch devices. Fixes: (1) Remove pointer-events:none from .shortcut-toast in App.css. (2) Add onClick={() => dismiss(true)} to the toast div. (3) Add to App.css: @media (max-width:480px) { .shortcut-toast { right:0.5rem; left:0.5rem; white-space:normal; text-align:center; font-size:0.68rem; bottom:3.5rem; } } to wrap text and lift above footer.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- VibeSparkline reads localStorage before parent writes -- always one state behind
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #7/#8/#9/#10. StatsBar useEffect reads vibeHistory from localStorage on vibe.key change. App.jsx vibe effect writes the new entry. React child useEffects run before parent useEffects in the same commit -- StatsBar reads the OLD value. Sparkline permanently lags by one vibe state. Fix: lift vibeHistory state to App.jsx. In the vibe effect after localStorage.setItem, call setVibeHistory(history). Pass vibeHistory as a prop to StatsBar. Remove the localStorage-reading useEffect from StatsBar entirely.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- StatsBar stuck worker scroll uses document.querySelector -- silently fails from Office tab
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #7/#8/#9/#10. App.jsx ~line 451: document.querySelector('[data-worker-name]')?.scrollIntoView(). WorkerCard nodes only exist when Dashboard tab is active. Clicking the stuck card from the Office tab does nothing with zero feedback. Fix: pass onWorkerClick as a prop to StatsBar from App.jsx. In the stuck card onClick call onWorkerClick(stuckWorkers[0]) instead of the querySelector hack. handleWorkerClick already handles tab switching and setsFocusedWorker; WorkerCard already handles scrollIntoView on focus. The infrastructure is all there -- just wire the prop.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- document.title cycles every 8s is disruptive for screen readers
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #8/#9/#10. App.jsx: title rotates through 3 frames every 8s via setInterval. Screen readers announce tab title changes -- audio interrupt every 8 seconds regardless of user activity. No prefers-reduced-motion guard, no document.hasFocus() check. Fix A (recommended): set document.title once on syncFromGitHub completion, remove interval entirely -- status info already lives in StatsBar. Fix B: guard with if (!window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches && document.hasFocus()) and clear on visibilitychange.

suggestions.title: NEW -- ARIA radio group keyboard pattern violated in both activity filter bars
suggestions.description: NEW IN REVIEW #11. App.jsx ~lines 1145-1175: two filter bars use role='radiogroup' with role='radio' buttons and aria-checked -- correct ARIA semantics. But keyboard implementation is wrong. ARIA radio groups require roving tabindex: only the checked item has tabIndex=0, all others get tabIndex=-1, arrow keys move between options. Currently ALL buttons receive default tabIndex=0, forcing keyboard users to tab through every filter button (6+ tab stops). Compare with the tab toggle which correctly implements roving tabindex. Fix: add tabIndex={activityFilter === key ? 0 : -1} in the type filter map and tabIndex={workerFilter === member.name ? 0 : -1} in the worker filter map. Add onKeyDown handlers for ArrowLeft/ArrowRight mirroring handleTabKeyDown.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- Header greeting badge overflow on slow-day vibe (latent, not triggered this run)
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #9/#10. Currently masked -- vibe is 'in-flow' (honorific = 'boss', 4 chars). But HONORIFICS['slow-day'] = 'chief... you still there?'. Badge text at slow-day: 'Good morning, chief... you still there? -- Sunday, Mar 8' -- ~380px at small font on a 375px viewport. App.css has white-space:nowrap on .header-badge with no max-width or overflow protection. Next slow-day trigger will clip the left edge. Fix: in App.css at the <=420px breakpoint add .header-badge { max-width: min(260px, 100%); overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; }.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- completionToast uses 15 inline styles including hardcoded box-shadow
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #8/#9/#10. App.jsx lines ~950-973: completion toast has 15 inline style properties. boxShadow hardcodes rgba(0,0,0,0.4) which looks wrong in light theme. Only border is genuinely dynamic. Fix: extract all static properties to .completion-toast class in App.css. Use a CSS custom property for the dynamic border: style={{ '--toast-border-color': completionToast.color }} and in CSS: border: 2px solid var(--toast-border-color); box-shadow: 0 4px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.4), 0 0 12px color-mix(in srgb, var(--toast-border-color) 33%, transparent). Also add role='status' alongside aria-live='polite'.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- getRelativeTime duplicated verbatim as inline IIFE in Office.jsx ghost bubble
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #7/#8/#9/#10. Office.jsx ~line 432: ghost bubble uses an inline IIFE with identical mins/hours/days arithmetic to getRelativeTime() in App.jsx lines 34-43. Any future format change must be applied in two places. Fix: create src/utils/time.js, export getRelativeTime (and move formatDuration, formatFullDateTime there too). Import in both App.jsx and Office.jsx, replacing the duplicate IIFE with a single getRelativeTime(lastEvent.timestamp) call.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- Idle wander and cloud-drift animations use left/top: layout-triggering at 60fps
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #8/#9/#10. (1) Office.css ~lines 706-731: all four @keyframes wander-N animate left and top percentage values, forcing browser full layout on every frame. With 4+ idle workers simultaneously this is 4x layout per frame. Fix: set each .char--wander-N with a base left/top position, then animate transform:translate(Xpx, Ypx) relative deltas -- GPU-composited, zero layout cost. (2) @keyframes cloud-drift animates from left:-20% to left:115% -- same issue. Fix: set .window-cloud { left:-20% } as base, animate transform:translateX(0) to transform:translateX(135%).

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- Fading cards all receive index={activeWorkers.length}: stagger lost on simultaneous departures
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #7/#8/#9/#10. App.jsx ~line 1112: every fading WorkerCard gets index={activeWorkers.length} -- same --i value for all fading workers. When 2+ workers depart simultaneously all fade at identical timing, stagger effect lost entirely. Fix: change .map((w) => to .map((w, fadingIdx) => and pass index={activeWorkers.length + fadingIdx}.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- ActivityLogEntry key uses string concatenation without delimiters: silent collision risk
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #8/#9/#10. App.jsx ~line 130: key={entry.timestamp + entry.worker + i}. Without separators timestamp='123'+worker='45'+i=0 gives '1234500'; timestamp='12345'+worker='0'+i=0 gives identical '1234500'. Silent React key collision. Fix: key={`${entry.timestamp}-${entry.worker ?? 'system'}-${i}`}.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- 19 ambient particle animations running permanently compound mobile jank
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEWS #7/#8/#9/#10. App.jsx: 12 ambient particles. Office.jsx: 7 more. All run permanent CSS animations. Combined with 4x layout-triggering wander animations and cloud-drift, compositor pressure is material on mid-range mobile during the 30s sync cycle. Fix: add @media (pointer:coarse) { .ambient-particle:nth-child(even) { display:none; } } in both App.css and Office.css to halve particle count on touch devices with zero visual impact on desktop.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- completionToast.color hex alpha suffix '55' fragile for non-6-digit colors
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #10. App.jsx ~line 964: boxShadow string appends literal '55' to completionToast.color assuming 6-digit #rrggbb format. WORKER_ROLE_COLORS are all 6-digit today so it works. But a future worker with a 3-digit hex, named color, or rgb() value silently produces invalid CSS. Fix: use color-mix(in srgb, ${completionToast.color} 33%, transparent). Becomes moot if completionToast inline-styles item is fixed first.

suggestions.title: NEW -- Dead updatedAt prop in ProgressBar: vestigial parameter misleads future readers
suggestions.description: NEW IN REVIEW #11. WorkerCard JSX passes updatedAt={worker.updated_at} to ProgressBar. Inside ProgressBar, updatedAt is declared in props but NEVER READ -- ETA calculation correctly uses startedAt only: const workingMs = startedAt ? Date.now() - new Date(startedAt).getTime() : null. This is vestigial dead code from an earlier implementation. Fix: remove updatedAt from ProgressBar prop destructuring and from all JSX call sites. Zero behaviour change.

suggestions.title: NEW -- Worker filter buttons silently disappear when workers.json arrives as a flat array
suggestions.description: NEW IN REVIEW #11. App.jsx syncFromGitHub: when workers.json is a flat array (not the {workers:[], roster:[]} shape), setRoster([]) is called. The worker filter bar maps over roster -- if empty, all per-worker filter buttons silently vanish with no indication filtering is unavailable. Fix: when Array.isArray(data), derive roster from the flat worker data: setRoster(data.map(w => ({ id: w.id, name: w.name, role: w.role, emoji: w.emoji }))). Filter buttons then always render regardless of API response shape.
